content:
Hi, I was running make with clippy, and I got an issue 

CLIPPY L rust/kernel.o
error[E0433]: failed to resolve: could not find `CoercePointee` in `marker`
   --> rust/kernel/list/arc.rs:162:66
    |
162 | #[cfg_attr(CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_COERCE_POINTEE, derive(core::marker::CoercePointee))]
    |                                                                  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ could not find `CoercePointee` in `marker`

error[E0433]: failed to resolve: could not find `CoercePointee` in `marker`
   --> rust/kernel/sync/arc.rs:129:66
    |
129 | #[cfg_attr(CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_COERCE_POINTEE, derive(core::marker::CoercePointee))]
    |                                                                  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ could not find `CoercePointee` in `marker`

error[E0433]: failed to resolve: could not find `CoercePointee` in `marker`
   --> rust/kernel/sync/arc.rs:503:66
    |
503 | #[cfg_attr(CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_COERCE_POINTEE, derive(core::marker::CoercePointee))]
    |                                                                  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ could not find `CoercePointee` in `marker`

error[E0635]: unknown feature `derive_coerce_pointee`
  --> rust/kernel/lib.rs:16:54
   |
16 | #![cfg_attr(CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_COERCE_POINTEE, feature(derive_coerce_pointee))]
   |                                                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

error: aborting due to 4 previous errors

I found this commit and checked my rustc version. I was on an old 1.84 nightly:
rustc 1.84.0-nightly (662180b34 2024-10-20)

I think it is a minor issue, fixed with a simple rustc update, but my old nightly rustc seems to have matched the RUSTC_VERSION condition without having the macro yet.
I do not know if it is worth doing anything about it, but, just in case, Ill ping @Ding Xiang Fei , @Fiona Behrens and @Alice Ryhl

This is just a downside of using version numbers for feature detection. There will be nightlies between the two releases that don't have the features added in that version. I don't think we should do anything about it.
Would it make sense to ask the rust compiler team for cfgs if a nightly feature is available? Then we could just match on those if they are populated by the compiler
That would work for this particular case, but a lot of the version numbers are for "is XYZ bug fixed yet?"

We could make it work by testing whether a particular feature works -- it wouldn't be the first time. But we are trying to get away from unstable features anyway...

At the moment, if one is using nightly, one should generally use the latest one. Even the latest nightly is best effort (and may not work for other reasons unrelated to the kernel).
By the way, thanks for reporting it @Timothy G.! I think we can consider it in the future, if we see the demand for it.
